text:
Two little mice lived in a cozy warren right in the middle of a lush forest. They woke each morning feeling happy just dreaming up all the things they could learn about the world around them. One sunny morning, they decided they wanted to visit the grand old owl's nest up in the tall chestnut tree.

As they hopped down the winding woodland path, they admired the dappled sunlight that danced on the bark. They giggled as they spotted the occasional butterfly hanging suspended on a petal.

There were mushrooms they didn't know existed, birds chirping tunes they couldn't name, and leaves that glowed softly at evening.

They eventually made it to the owl's nest. The big owl, wise and grumpy all at once, was sitting on a large branch, glaring down on them.

"What do you want?" he barked, his old eyes squinting suspiciously.

"To play?" the smallest mouse ventured. He blinked sleepily, half awake.

The owl narrowed
